verdict_reasoning: TMED9 is a type I transmembrane cargo receptor (GOLD domain + coiled-coil domain + single TM helix + cytoplasmic tail) whose dominant localization is the ER, ERGIC, and cis-Golgi, where it functions in COPI/COPII vesicle trafficking and quality control of misfolded proteins. Its luminal N-terminal domain faces the Golgi/ER lumen (topologically equivalent to extracellular at the PM). Published literature documents that TMED9 is found at the plasma membrane as a minor pool during its constitutive secretory cycling (Hasegawa et al., 2010; Li et al., 2022), placing it firmly in the dual_localization bucket: it has its own TM anchor and does transiently reach the PM outer leaflet, but the PM is not its dominant or stable compartment. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic (antibody, ADC, CAR-T) specifically targets TMED9 on the outer PM leaflet. No documented cancer ecto-pool, cell-death surface display, narrow germline/developmental restriction, lysosomal exocytosis mechanism, or wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to the outer PM is reported. The contextual surface access is via secretory cycling of this endomembrane-resident TM protein.
predicted_key_uncertainty: The PM pool is documented but very minor and transient; no quantitative surface biotinylation or flow cytometry data on intact live cells confirming outer-leaflet accessibility of TMED9 at the PM was found.
